name: Chicken Adobo
description: Tender chicken simmered in soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, and bay-style seasoning for a classic savory ulam.
steps: Saute garlic and onion until fragrant.
Add chicken and lightly brown the pieces.
Pour in soy sauce, vinegar, and a little water.
Simmer until the chicken is cooked and the sauce reduces.

name: Pork Adobo
description: Pork cubes slowly cooked in a tangy soy-vinegar sauce until rich and tender.
steps: Saute garlic and onion.
Add pork and cook until the edges brown.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, and water.
Simmer until tender and reduce the sauce to taste.

name: Sinigang na Baboy
description: Comforting sour pork soup with vegetables, perfect with hot rice.
steps: Boil pork with onion and tomato until tender.
Season the broth with a souring base and fish sauce.
Add firm vegetables first, then leafy greens.
Serve while hot with rice.

name: Sinigang na Bangus
description: Bangus cooked in a bright sour broth with tomato and simple vegetables.
steps: Bring water, onion, and tomato to a simmer.
Add bangus and season the broth.
Add vegetables and cook briefly.
Adjust sourness and saltiness before serving.

name: Tinolang Manok
description: Chicken soup with sayote and leafy greens in a light ginger-style broth.
steps: Saute aromatics, then add chicken.
Season with fish sauce and add water.
Simmer until chicken is cooked.
Add sayote and greens near the end.

name: Ginataang Kalabasa at Sitaw
description: Kalabasa and sitaw simmered in coconut milk for a creamy vegetable ulam.
steps: Saute garlic and onion.
Add kalabasa, sitaw, and coconut milk.
Simmer until vegetables are tender.
Season and serve when the sauce thickens slightly.

name: Tortang Talong
description: Flattened roasted eggplant dipped in beaten egg and pan-fried until golden.
steps: Soften the eggplant, then peel and flatten it.
Beat eggs with simple seasoning.
Dip eggplant in egg mixture.
Pan-fry both sides until set and golden.

name: Ginisang Ampalaya with Egg
description: Ampalaya sauteed with tomato and egg for a quick, everyday vegetable dish.
steps: Saute garlic, onion, and tomato.
Add sliced ampalaya and cook until just tender.
Pour in beaten egg.
Stir gently and season to taste.

name: Pinakbet
description: A mix of Filipino vegetables simmered with savory seasoning and optional pork or shrimp.
steps: Saute onion and tomato.
Add vegetables that need longer cooking first.
Season and simmer until almost tender.
Add delicate vegetables last to avoid overcooking.

name: Bicol Express
description: Pork cooked in coconut milk with bold, spicy, and savory flavors.
steps: Saute garlic and onion, then add pork.
Pour in coconut milk and simmer.
Season with fish sauce and chili if available.
Cook until pork is tender and sauce is creamy.

name: Pork Menudo
description: Pork stew with tomato-based sauce and pantry-friendly vegetables.
steps: Marinate pork briefly with soy sauce if time allows.
Saute garlic, onion, and tomato.
Add pork and simmer with sauce.
Cook until pork is tender and flavors come together.

name: Chicken Afritada
description: Chicken simmered in tomato sauce-style seasoning for a homey rice pairing.
steps: Saute garlic, onion, and tomato.
Add chicken and cook until lightly browned.
Simmer with sauce and a little water.
Cook until chicken is tender and sauce is balanced.

name: Paksiw na Isda
description: Fish gently cooked in vinegar, garlic, and seasoning for a sharp, simple ulam.
steps: Arrange fish with garlic and onion in a pan.
Add vinegar, water, and seasoning.
Simmer without stirring too much.
Add vegetables if using and cook until done.

name: Bangus Sisig
description: Flaked bangus sauteed with aromatics for a savory, sizzling-style dish.
steps: Cook bangus, then flake the meat.
Saute garlic and onion.
Add bangus flakes and seasoning.
Finish with egg or tomato if available.

name: Adobong Kangkong
description: Kangkong cooked adobo-style with soy sauce, vinegar, and garlic.
steps: Saute garlic and onion.
Add kangkong stems first, then leaves.
Pour in soy sauce and vinegar.
Cook briefly so the greens stay bright.

name: Ginisang Pechay
description: Pechay quickly sauteed with garlic, onion, and tomato for a light vegetable ulam.
steps: Saute garlic, onion, and tomato.
Add pechay stems, then leaves.
Season lightly.
Cook just until tender.

name: Sardines with Misua
description: Canned sardines stretched into a warm noodle soup for a quick simple meal.
steps: Saute garlic, onion, and tomato.
Add sardines and water.
Add misua noodles if available.
Simmer briefly and adjust seasoning.

name: Corned Beef Guisado
description: Corned beef sauteed with garlic, onion, and tomato for a fast breakfast or lunch ulam.
steps: Saute garlic, onion, and tomato.
Add corned beef and break it up.
Cook until heated through and lightly toasted.
Serve with rice or egg if available.

name: Tokwa't Baboy
description: Fried tofu and pork tossed with a tangy soy-vinegar onion sauce.
steps: Fry tofu until crisp, then cut into pieces.
Cook pork until tender, then slice.
Mix soy sauce, vinegar, onion, and seasoning.
Toss tofu and pork with the sauce before serving.

name: Shrimp Ginataan
description: Shrimp simmered in coconut milk with aromatics for a creamy seafood dish.
steps: Saute garlic and onion.
Add coconut milk and simmer gently.
Add shrimp and cook until just done.
Season and add vegetables if available.

name: Adobong Sitaw
description: Adobong Sitaw quickly sauteed with aromatics for an everyday Filipino ulam.
steps: Saute garlic and onion until fragrant.
Add the main ingredient and stir-fry.
Season with pantry condiments.
Cook just until tender and serve.

name: Adobong Pusit
description: Adobong Pusit gently simmered until the main ingredients absorb the savory sauce.
steps: Saute aromatics until fragrant.
Add the main ingredient and sauce.
Simmer until cooked and flavors blend.
Reduce the sauce to your preferred consistency.

steps: Saute aromatics until fragrant.

steps: Saute aromatics until fragrant.
